title: elymus trachycaulus subsp. andinus
intro: Elymus trachycaulus subsp. andinus, also known as the Andean wild rye, is a plant species that belongs to the Poaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing the risk of chronic diseases such as heart disease and diabetes, as well as improving digestion and reducing stress levels. The herbal preparations of Elymus trachycaulus subsp. andinus can be made into teas, infusions, or extracts, and are often used in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being and improve immune function.
